steps.title: Find the contrast signal
steps.content: The sentence says Greco-Roman sculpture is "sometimes characterized as idealizing human subjects, but in fact there are many" that depict people in decidedly ______ ways. The phrase "but in fact" is a contrast signal: the blank must be the opposite of idealizing — something that means depicting people negatively or unflatteringly rather than beautifully.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice and pick the match
steps.content:
• Choice A — superficial: Means shallow or lacking depth. This describes the quality of an observation, not whether a depiction is flattering or unflattering. It doesn't contrast with "idealizing." ✗
  • Choice B — implausible: Means unbelievable or unrealistic. This targets believability, not the flattering-vs-unflattering contrast the passage sets up. ✗
  • Choice C — unflattering: Means presenting someone in a negative or unappealing way — the direct opposite of idealizing, which portrays subjects beautifully. ✓
  • Choice D — imprecise: Means inaccurate or vague. This targets accuracy, not the aesthetic praise-vs-criticism contrast signaled by "idealizing." ✗
C is correct.
